content.full_text:
Fern PollrrcAl PnecrrcEs Corr,rMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
Iuly 16,2003
Margie L. Rice, Mayor
City of Westminster
Civic Center
8200 Westminster Boulevard
Westminster, CA 92683
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-03-140
Dear Ms. Rice:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,).r
QUESTION
Must amounts in Column B of the Summary Page of the Recipient Committee
Campaign Statement, Form 460 include Column B totals from the previous statement
filed?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Cumulative totals are calculated on a calendar year basis. The total amounts
on Form 460, SummaryPage, Column B from any previous report must be added to
figures in Column A for the report being filed to determine the totals for Column B,
unless the report being filed covers the period beginning January 1. In that case, Column
B totals from the previous report should not be added to figures in Column A, except for
line 2 (Loans Received), line 7 (Loans Made), and line 9 (Accrued Expenses).
FACTS
You were a candidate in the general municipal election held in the City of
Westminster on November 5,2002. Your controlled committee filed semi-annual and
pre-election campaign statements in connection with the election.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@


File No. A-03-140
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
Section 84211 (a) and (b) require committees to report, in part:
"The total amount of contributions received [and expenditures made]
during the period covered by the campaign statement and the total
cumulative amount of contributions received [and expenditures made]."
Section 82018 defines "cumulative amount" to mean "the amount of contributions
received or expenditures made in the calend,ar year." 2 Thus, when reporting
contributions received on Form 460, Schedules A, B, and C, contributions received from
one source must be cumulated with contributions received from the same source in all
subsequent reports covering the same calendar year. The Form 460 Summary page was
designed with two columns to allow for cumulative reporting: Column A is for the
committee to report totals during the period covered by the campaign statement; and,
column B is for the committee to report totals for the calendar year.
Every Jarnary 1, cumulation begins again. However, section 8a2n@) and
regulation 18421.6 require the reporting of outstanding loans made or received, and
accrued expenses, to be carried forward from one reporting period to the next, including
from one calendar year to the next. This carry over is reported on Schedules B, F, and H,
and on the Summary Page, Column B, lines 2,7, and 9 of the Form 460.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Political Reform Division
cc: 
Marian Contreras, City Clerk
KSMjg

By:
2-' lhere rs an exception to calendar year cumulation when a pre-election campaign statement is
filed in one year in connection with a local or special election held in another year. In that case, the period
over which the cumulative amount is calculated ends on the closing date of the semi-annual statement or
report filed after the election. (Section 82018(b) and (d).) This occurs when an election is held in January
or early February.
